calibration_finding: Short UI copy (8-25 words) is not a valid target for AI detection APIs. Originality.ai states explicitly: texts under 50 words produce unreliable results. GPTZero also treats terse command-style sentences as AI-generated regardless of authorship. All 7 snippets tested are under 35 words. The API tools are calibrated for 100+ word body paragraphs (coaching notes, LLM-generated Today's Focus text, cover letter paragraphs). Short UI labels and status messages should be validated through human readability review (grade level + plain-language check), not AI detection APIs.
recommendation: Reserve assertHumanLikePassesAi() for LLM-generated body-copy paragraphs ≥ 50 words. Do not run it on UI labels, status messages, or one-sentence callouts. The gradeLevel() + humanizeBody() helpers are the right tools for short copy.
gptzero_short_text_finding: GPTZero returns prob=1 for the classic typing test phrase 'The quick brown fox...' (26 words). This confirms the detector is unreliable on short text regardless of actual authorship.

summary.root_cause: All snippets are under 35 words. AI detection APIs require 50+ words for reliable results. Short UI copy cannot be meaningfully validated this way.
summary.action: Use assertHumanLikePassesAi() only on ≥50-word LLM body paragraphs. Use gradeLevel() for short copy quality gating.
